title: Variables

contentBlocks.text: A variable is a labeled box that stores a value so you can use it later. You create one by giving it a name and using the = sign.
Notice there are no quotation marks around 12 — that's because it's a number, not text.
Create a variable named score, set it equal to the number 100, and then print it.

starterCode:
# Create a variable called score set to 100
# Then print it

testCases.successMessage: score is set to exactly the right value!
testCases.failureMessage: Make sure you have a variable named score set to 100.
Don't forget to print(score) so we can see the value.

hints: Then, on its own line, print it: print(score)
